note: Explorer address dashboard: clarify gross address flow accounting. Self-send TXs (capsule anchors, consolidations to the same address, change outputs returning to source) were inflating the lifetime SENT figure without a clear counterweight, which made the dashboard look like the address had paid out more than it actually had. Three layout changes (display-only, no RPC schema or consensus change): (1) Rename the existing snapshot row to '↓ RECEIVED · currently held (non-mining)' so it is visibly a snapshot and not a lifetime aggregate. (2) Add two new lifetime rows side by side — '↓ GROSS received (lifetime)' and '↑ GROSS sent (lifetime)' — both hydrated from the same getaddressflows RPC call. The pair makes the symmetric structure obvious: a capsule self-send of N SOST inflates both rows by N without affecting balance. (3) Add a '⊕ NET external outflow' row labeled 'not computed in v0 · see per-TX flow for breakdown' so a reader does not assume the gross sent figure is the real external outflow. Plus a cyan ℹ inline note directly below the three flow rows that spells out the self-send caveat in plain English. The existing snapshot row's tooltip now also surfaces both lifetime numbers when hovered. NO consensus / RPC / node / miner / wallet / wire-format change. NO new endpoints. Pure JS + HTML display layer in sost-explorer.html showAddress.
